| [sound:mobile.mp3] | [sound:mobile_meaning.mp3] | [sound:mobile_example.mp3] | If something is mobile, it can be moved easily. | mobile phones are popular because you can take them anywhere. | ˈmoʊbl

| [sound:pollute.mp3] | [sound:pollute_meaning.mp3] | [sound:pollute_example.mp3] | To pollute means to make air, water, or land dirty, unclean, or foul. | The careless factory polluted the river with chemicals. | pəˈluːt

| [sound:transportation.mp3] | [sound:transportation_meaning.mp3] | [sound:transportation_example.mp3] | transportation is any type of vehicle that can carry people or things. | I don’t have a car, so my normal transportation is the train. | ˌtrænspɔːrˈteɪʃn

| [sound:bill.mp3] | [sound:bill_meaning.mp3] | [sound:bill_example.mp3] | A bill is a statement of money owed for goods or a service. | I have so many bills that I do not know how to pay for them all. | bɪl

| [sound:chaos.mp3] | [sound:chaos_meaning.mp3] | [sound:chaos_example.mp3] | chaos is a situation that is confusing and not ordered. | His presentation was in chaos. I couldn’t understand what he meant. | ˈkeɪɑːs

| [sound:cyclone.mp3] | [sound:cyclone_meaning.mp3] | [sound:cyclone_example.mp3] | A cyclone is a large storm with heavy rain and winds that spin in a circle. | Hundreds of homes were damaged by the cyclone. | ˈsaɪkloʊn

| [sound:doomed.mp3] | [sound:doomed_meaning.mp3] | [sound:doomed_example.mp3] | If someone or something is doomed, they are going to fail or be destroyed. | Since I spent all my money, my date with Jane is doomed.

| [sound:heir.mp3] | [sound:heir_meaning.mp3] | [sound:heir_example.mp3] | An heir is a person who receives money or property of someone who dies. | The princess was the heir to the king and queen’s throne. | er

| [sound:poultry.mp3] | [sound:poultry_meaning.mp3] | [sound:poultry_example.mp3] | poultry is a bird, such as a chicken, that is used for meat and eggs. | He raises poultry and sells their meat for extra money. | ˈpoʊltri

| [sound:sergeant.mp3] | [sound:sergeant_meaning.mp3] | [sound:sergeant_example.mp3] | A sergeant is a soldier or police officer of middle rank. | He was promoted to sergeant after a year in the army. | ˈsɑːrdʒənt

| [sound:sheer.mp3] | [sound:sheer_meaning.mp3] | [sound:sheer_example.mp3] | If you describe something as sheer, it is complete and very strong. | I was impressed by her sheer dedication to jogging. | ʃɪr

| [sound:textile.mp3] | [sound:textile_meaning.mp3] | [sound:textile_example.mp3] | textile is cloth that has been woven or knitted. | The blue textile was going to be used to make blouses. | ˈtekstaɪl

| [sound:tornado.mp3] | [sound:tornado_meaning.mp3] | [sound:tornado_example.mp3] | A tornado is a tube-shaped formation of air that spins very quickly. | During a tornado, the safest place to be is underground. | tɔːrˈneɪdoʊ
